title: evidence-brief input spec
description:
Input spec for ``evidence-brief`` — the POINT query (E5, primary).

Keyed by ``tags`` (scope-tag membership) AND/OR ``lineage`` (a ``run_id``
whose command-identity chain selects code-identical work — the always-present
fallback that needs no human to have tagged anything). At LEAST ONE key is
required: an unkeyed point query is the recorded browse NON-GOAL (the agent
is the browser; there is no faceted explorer). Both may be given; the union
is disclosed per-source in the result.

tags.description: Scope-tag slugs to select by (membership; a record matches when it carries any queried tag OR a current conclusion retro-indexes it under one). Opaque caller data — core never interprets a tag's meaning.
tags.title: Tags

lineage.description: A run_id whose lineage-chain + cmd_sha select by CODE IDENTITY — the tag-free fallback. Null when the query is tag-keyed only.
lineage.title: Lineage

as_of.description: Optional ISO-8601 timestamp: the collector includes only records with ts <= as_of, so the digest is 'what was known as of that date'. A timestamp, never a named period ('2025H1' is caller vocabulary).
as_of.title: As Of

fleet.description: When False (default), scope is the single experiment_dir. When True, run the identical per-namespace walk over every experiment this machine has journaled (glob '*/repo.json' under the journal home); a torn/unreadable namespace is skipped and counted in 'skipped'.
fleet.title: Fleet
